metadata.archetype_justification: Three microservices (product, basket, ordering) each own DynamoDB tables and expose CRUD endpoints via API Gateway. The dominant pattern is stateful CRUD operations on business entities. Classified as stateful-crud.
metadata.context: Event-driven serverless microservices (product, basket, ordering) with Lambda, DynamoDB, EventBridge, SQS. The agent will invoke these as tools for order status lookups and return processing.

top_gaps.title: CI/CD Automation
top_gaps.gap: No CI/CD pipeline — all deployments manual
top_gaps.impact: Blocks automated delivery; triggers Modern DevOps pathway

top_gaps.title: Deployment Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No deployment strategy — direct manual cdk deploy
top_gaps.impact: No safety net for production releases; risk of outages

top_gaps.title: Integration Testing
top_gaps.gap: No automated tests — test file is placeholder
top_gaps.impact: Cannot validate system behavior; regression risk on changes

top_gaps.title: API Authentication
top_gaps.gap: API Gateway endpoints completely open — no auth
top_gaps.impact: Security vulnerability; unauthorized access to all APIs

top_gaps.title: Backup and Recovery
top_gaps.gap: No backups on DynamoDB; RemovalPolicy.DESTROY set
top_gaps.impact: Data loss risk; no recovery capability

findings.title: Workflow Orchestration — checkout flow lacks orchestration
findings.description: The checkout flow is a multi-step business operation (validate basket → publish event → delete basket) implemented directly in the basket Lambda handler with no dedicated workflow orchestration service.
findings.gap: No error handling for partial failures in checkout. If EventBridge publish succeeds but basket delete fails, there is no compensation logic.
findings.recommendation: Implement the checkout flow as a Step Functions state machine with error handling and compensation at each step.

findings.title: Network Security — API Gateway publicly exposed without WAF
findings.description: API Gateway endpoints are publicly accessible without WAF protection or resource policies. Lambda uses IAM for service access (correct for serverless) but the edge layer lacks protection.
findings.gap: No WAF, no resource policies, no IP restrictions on API Gateway. No managed networking services layered on.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS WAF to API Gateway stages. Configure resource policies. Consider API Gateway usage plans with throttling.

findings.title: API Entry Point — no request validation or explicit throttling
findings.description: API Gateway REST APIs serve as entry points with route-based configuration and default throttling, but lack explicit throttling config, request validation, and authentication.
findings.gap: No request validators/models. No usage plans. No authentication on gateway.
findings.recommendation: Configure API Gateway request validators and models. Add usage plans with API keys for throttling. Add authorizers.

findings.title: Auto-Scaling — no Lambda concurrency controls
findings.description: Lambda and DynamoDB on-demand provide inherent auto-scaling, but no explicit concurrency controls or capacity alarms are configured.
findings.gap: No Lambda reserved concurrency (runaway invocation risk). No DynamoDB consumed-capacity alarms. No provisioned concurrency for cold starts.
findings.recommendation: Configure reserved concurrency on Lambda functions. Add CloudWatch alarms on DynamoDB consumed capacity. Consider provisioned concurrency for latency-sensitive endpoints.

findings.title: Backup and Recovery — no backups, RemovalPolicy.DESTROY
findings.description: No backup configuration on any DynamoDB table. Tables use RemovalPolicy.DESTROY. No PITR enabled. No AWS Backup plans.
findings.gap: All three DynamoDB tables have no PITR, no backup plans, and RemovalPolicy.DESTROY — production data loss risk.
findings.recommendation: Enable DynamoDB PITR. Change removalPolicy to RETAIN. Consider AWS Backup for scheduled backups.

findings.title: IaC Coverage — no operational resources in IaC
findings.description: All primary resources in CDK but no operational/DR resources: no CloudWatch alarms, dashboards, backup plans, health checks, or DLQs.
findings.gap: Operational resources (monitoring, alerting, backups) are not codified.
findings.recommendation: Extend CDK to include CloudWatch alarms, dashboards, DynamoDB PITR, and Lambda DLQs.

findings.title: CI/CD Automation — no pipeline exists
findings.description: No CI/CD pipeline in the repository. No .github/workflows, buildspec.yml, Jenkinsfile, or CodePipeline. All deployments are manual cdk deploy.
findings.gap: All deployments manual. No automated build, test, or deploy stages. No quality gates. No rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement CI/CD pipeline with CDK Pipelines or GitHub Actions. Include lint, build, test, synth, deploy-staging, integration-test, deploy-production stages.

findings.title: Programming Languages — EOL Node.js 14.x runtime
findings.description: Node.js 14.x (EOL Nov 2023), TypeScript 3.9.7, CDK 2.17.0. AWS SDK v3 is current but runtime and framework versions create compound legacy signals.
findings.gap: EOL runtime (no security patches). Outdated CDK and TypeScript versions.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Lambda runtime to Node.js 20.x+. Upgrade CDK to latest 2.x. Upgrade TypeScript to 5.x.

findings.title: API Versioning — no versioning strategy
findings.description: No API versioning. Routes are /product, /basket, /order with no version prefix, headers, or annotations.
findings.gap: No versioning mechanism. Breaking changes affect all consumers simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Implement URL-based versioning (/v1/product, /v1/basket, /v1/order). Critical for agent tool invocation stability.

findings.title: Service Discovery — hardcoded configuration via env vars
findings.description: Table names and EventBridge bus name passed via environment variables set at deploy time. No dynamic discovery or service registry.
findings.gap: Configuration hardcoded in CDK. No API catalog. No dynamic service discovery.
findings.recommendation: Use SSM Parameter Store or AppConfig for service configuration. Consider EventBridge schema registry for event contract discovery.

findings.title: Data Access Layer — no repository/DAO pattern
findings.description: DynamoDB commands constructed inline in handler functions. ddbClient.js only initializes the client instance — no abstraction over data operations.
findings.gap: Data access logic scattered in handlers. No repository pattern for testability or maintainability.
findings.recommendation: Extract data access into repository modules per service (productRepository.js, basketRepository.js, orderRepository.js).

findings.title: Encryption at Rest — AWS-owned keys only, no CMK
findings.description: DynamoDB uses default AWS-owned encryption. No customer-managed KMS keys. SQS queue uses default encryption.
findings.gap: No customer-managed KMS keys. Cannot control key rotation or audit key access.
findings.recommendation: Add customer-managed KMS keys for DynamoDB tables and SQS queue. Configure key rotation.

findings.title: API Authentication — endpoints completely open
findings.description: No authentication on any API Gateway endpoint. All REST APIs open to the public internet. No authorizers, API keys, or usage plans.
findings.gap: All API endpoints unauthenticated. Unauthorized access to all CRUD operations.
findings.recommendation: Add Cognito User Pool or Lambda authorizer to all API Gateway methods. Configure API keys with usage plans.

findings.title: Centralized Identity — no identity provider
findings.description: No identity provider integration. No Cognito, OIDC, or SAML. The application has no authentication at all.
findings.gap: No identity management. Cannot attribute actions to users or enforce access policies.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Amazon Cognito as centralized IdP. Create User Pool with OAuth2/OIDC flows. Configure API Gateway Cognito authorizers.

findings.title: Compute Hardening — EOL runtime, no scanning
findings.description: Lambda runtime Node.js 14.x is EOL (no security patches). No vulnerability scanning. No dependency scanning. AWS SDK packages outdated.
findings.gap: EOL runtime. No vulnerability or dependency scanning. Outdated SDK packages.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to Node.js 20.x+. Add Dependabot or Snyk. Update AWS SDK packages. Consider Lambda runtime management controls.

findings.title: Security Pipeline — no security scanning
findings.description: No CI/CD pipeline, therefore no security scanning. No SAST, no dependency scanning, no Dependabot.
findings.gap: No security validation in development workflow. Vulnerabilities reach production undetected.
findings.recommendation: Integrate npm audit/Snyk for dependencies, Semgrep/CodeGuru for SAST, and CDK-nag for IaC security when CI/CD is implemented.

findings.title: Distributed Tracing — not instrumented
findings.description: No X-Ray active tracing on Lambda. No OpenTelemetry. No trace propagation across EventBridge→SQS→Lambda flow.
findings.gap: Cannot trace requests across microservices. Cross-service debugging is guesswork.
findings.recommendation: Enable X-Ray active tracing on all Lambda functions and API Gateway stages. Consider ADOT Lambda layer.

findings.title: SLO Definitions — none defined
findings.description: No SLO definitions. No CloudWatch alarms on latency or error rates. No error budget tracking.
findings.gap: No formal SLOs for critical user journeys.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs: Product API p99 < 200ms, Checkout success > 99.5%, Order query p99 < 500ms. Implement as CloudWatch alarms.

findings.title: Business Metrics — none published
findings.description: No custom business metrics. No CloudWatch putMetricData calls. Only default Lambda metrics available.
findings.gap: No visibility into business outcomes (orders placed, checkout rates, product catalog health).
findings.recommendation: Add custom metrics using CloudWatch Embedded Metric Format: orders-placed, checkout-success/failure, products-created.

findings.title: Anomaly Detection — no alerting configured
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms. No anomaly detection. No PagerDuty/OpsGenie/SNS integration.
findings.gap: No automated failure detection. Incidents discovered by users.
findings.recommendation: Add alarms: Lambda error rate, duration p99, DynamoDB throttles, SQS DLQ count. Add SNS notification topic.

findings.title: Deployment Strategy — direct manual deploy
findings.description: No deployment strategy. Manual cdk deploy directly to production. No canary, blue/green, or traffic shifting.
findings.gap: Bad deployments affect all users immediately with no rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement Lambda canary deployments with AutoPublishAlias and DeploymentPreference. Add CloudFormation rollback triggers.

findings.title: Integration Testing — no tests implemented
findings.description: Only test file is a placeholder with all assertions commented out. No integration, API, or contract tests.
findings.gap: No automated testing. Cannot validate infrastructure, API behavior, or event flows.
findings.recommendation: Implement CDK assertion tests, API integration tests, and EventBridge flow tests using Jest.

findings.title: Incident Response — entirely ad hoc
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation, no self-healing. No DLQs on Lambda functions.
findings.gap: No documented or automated incident response procedures.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common scenarios. Add Lambda DLQs. Consider SSM Automation for remediation.

findings.title: Observability Ownership — no ownership defined
findings.description: No per-service dashboards, no named alarm owners, no CODEOWNERS, no team attribution.
findings.gap: No clear ownership of service health.
findings.recommendation: Add CODEOWNERS. Create per-service CloudWatch dashboards in CDK. Tag resources with team ownership.

findings.title: Resource Tagging — no tags on any resources
findings.description: No tags on DynamoDB tables, Lambda functions, API Gateways, EventBridge, or SQS. No cost allocation or ownership tags.
findings.gap: Cannot track costs per service, identify ownership, or distinguish environments.
findings.recommendation: Add CDK stack-level tags (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter) using Tags.of(). Use CDK Aspects for enforcement.

evaluations.title: Managed Compute
evaluations.reason: All compute on Lambda (serverless). No EC2.

evaluations.title: Async Messaging and Streaming
evaluations.reason: EventBridge + SQS for cross-service async; sync reads via API Gateway appropriate for stateful-crud.

evaluations.title: High Availability and Fault Isolation
evaluations.reason: All services (Lambda, DynamoDB, API GW, EventBridge, SQS) are inherently multi-AZ.

evaluations.title: Monolith vs Microservices
evaluations.reason: Three independently deployable microservices with separate databases and async communication.

evaluations.title: Async vs Sync Communication
evaluations.reason: Cross-service communication 100% async via EventBridge+SQS. Sync for client-facing CRUD (correct).

evaluations.title: Long-Running Process Handling
evaluations.reason: No operations exceed 30s. All DynamoDB calls sub-second. Checkout async by design.

evaluations.title: Unstructured Data Storage
evaluations.reason: System handles only structured data in DynamoDB. No unstructured data requirements.

evaluations.title: Database Engine Version and EOL
evaluations.reason: DynamoDB is serverless with no user-managed engine versions. No EOL risk.

evaluations.title: Stored Procedures and Schema Complexity
evaluations.reason: DynamoDB has no stored procedures. All logic in application layer.

evaluations.title: Audit Logging
evaluations.reason: Application-level IaC only. CloudTrail is account-level, belongs in foundation repo.

evaluations.title: Secrets Management
evaluations.reason: No secrets needed. All AWS access via IAM roles. Environment vars contain only non-secret config.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2 = 4 — already microservices with well-defined boundaries. Primary trigger (APP-Q2 < 3) not met.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1 = 4 — compute already on Lambda (serverless). Contextual guard: workload is Lambda/serverless, not EC2/VM.

triggering_questions.note: Compute is serverless (Lambda); threshold not met and guard blocks

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload already runs on Lambda (serverless). Container pathway does not apply to serverless-first architectures.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 = 4 — no stored procedures or proprietary SQL. No commercial database engines detected.

triggering_questions.note: No proprietary SQL; threshold not met
All databases managed; threshold not met

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 = 4 — managed messaging (EventBridge, SQS) in use. No data processing workloads exist.

triggering_questions.note: Managed messaging in use; threshold not met

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, or data processing workloads found. Managed analytics not applicable.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q11 = 1 (no CI/CD automation). Supporting: OPS-Q5 = 1, OPS-Q6 = 1.

triggering_questions.note: No CI/CD pipeline — primary trigger met
No deployment strategy — supporting trigger met
No integration tests — supporting trigger met

detail.current_state: No CI/CD pipeline. Manual cdk deploy. No deployment strategy. No automated tests.
immediate_actions: Implement GitHub Actions or CDK Pipelines CI/CD
Add CDK assertion tests and API integration tests
Add CloudWatch alarms and dashboards to CDK

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. AI signal terms not found in context field.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context mentions 'agent' but not AI-specific signal terms (agentic, LLM, Bedrock, generative AI, etc.).
